subject: English
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the theme of choices and their consequences
Analyze the poet’s tone and use of imagery
Identify poetic devices such as metaphor, rhyme, and symbolism
Interpret the meaning of stanzas and lines in depth
Relate the poem’s message to personal life and critical thinking

lesson_plan.title: Introduction and Background
topics_covered: About Robert Frost and his poetry
Context and inspiration for the poem
Overview of the theme of choices
teaching_strategy: Brainstorm initial ideas about choices and paths
student_activities: Share personal experiences of making choices

lesson_plan.title: Detailed Analysis: Stanza 1
teaching_strategy: Ask students to visualize the scene
student_activities: Paraphrase stanza in their own words

lesson_plan.title: Stanzas 2 and 3: Choices and Reflection
topics_covered: Analysis of poet’s reasoning for choosing a path
Symbolism of roads as life choices
Poetic devices: alliteration, rhyme, and figurative language
teaching_strategy: Identify poetic devices and explain their effect
student_activities: Discuss the significance of each choice in pairs

lesson_plan.title: Stanza 4: Moral and Reflection
topics_covered: Interpretation of 'I took the one less travelled by'
teaching_strategy: Critical thinking exercises on decisions and consequences

lesson_plan.title: Revision and Recap
topics_covered: Review of poetic devices and imagery

teaching_aids: Charts illustrating poetic devices and examples
teacher_tip: Encourage students to relate the poem to their personal experiences with decisions, helping them understand the metaphor of roads and life choices deeply.
